meta.author: Erika Flowers
meta.notes: Generated from JTBD Canvas exercise. Reviewed and confirmed by product owner.

job_statement: When I am deep in a draft and need to reference something I established hundreds of pages ago, I need a way to query my own canon instantly, so I can keep writing without breaking flow or contradicting myself.
functional_outcomes: Find all scenes where two specific characters interact
Retrieve what was established about a location, weapon, or faction across all books
Identify every mention of a plot thread to check for dangling setups
Get a structured timeline of events without re-reading entire manuscripts
Search by concept, not just keyword — 'scenes where trust is broken' not just 'trust'
See all aliases and references to a single entity resolved to one identity
emotional_outcomes: Feel confident that the draft is internally consistent
Reduce the anxiety of 'did I already contradict this?'
Experience flow state without interruption for manual lookups
Trust that the tool knows the canon as well as the author does
Feel like the tool respects the craft, not just the output
social_outcomes: Ship a book that readers trust for consistency and depth
Avoid embarrassing continuity errors caught by beta readers or reviewers
Demonstrate professional-grade worldbuilding to agents, editors, and publishers
Build a body of work that rewards close reading
pain_points: Ctrl+F across 400K words is not a strategy
Scrivener's notes and keywords do not scale past one book
Personal wikis go stale the moment the draft changes
Re-reading your own manuscripts to check a fact kills momentum
Existing AI writing tools want to write FOR you, not help you write BETTER
No tool understands that 'Samantha' in Chapter 3 and 'Agent Mori' in Chapter 40 are the same person

current_alternatives.name: Scrivener notes
current_alternatives.failure: Manual, does not scale, no search across books

current_alternatives.name: Personal wiki (Notion, Obsidian)
current_alternatives.failure: Goes stale, separate from manuscript, manual sync

current_alternatives.name: Generic AI chatbots
current_alternatives.failure: No knowledge of YOUR canon, hallucinate details

current_alternatives.name: Memory alone
current_alternatives.failure: Breaks past ~100K words for most authors

success_metrics: Zero continuity errors caught by beta readers
Time from 'I need to check something' to answer: under 10 seconds
Author can draft a full chapter without leaving the writing environment
Canon references are clickable, navigable, always current
New scenes automatically update the knowledge base
